codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses the blocking issue from admin-dashboard-redesign. The listBookings function now fetches service_request, procedureType, and citizen user data, populating booking.request with the fields needed for the Recent Appointments list: citizen name and procedure type name.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The blocking issue was in backend service layer (bookings-read.service.ts). The fix adds three sequential DB queries per booking (service_request, procedure_type, user) for bookings that have a requestId. While this is correct, it could be a performance concern for large lists. However, since admin.bookings.list already does per-booking queries for slot and staffUser, this pattern was already established.

originalFailureIssues: booking.request was always undefined because admin.bookings.list only populated slot and staff relations
booking.request?.citizen?.name fell through to staff.email (wrong data)
fixAddressesIssues: serviceReq is fetched when booking.requestId exists - provides email and citizenUserId
citizenUser is fetched via serviceReq.citizenUserId - provides name for citizen.name
procedureType is fetched via serviceReq.procedureTypeId - provides name for procedureType.name
summary: Fix correctly resolves all three blocking issues from admin-dashboard-redesign. The dashboard Recent Appointments list will now show actual citizen names and procedure type names. No new issues introduced.
